angle: Use the routing architecture tool to ask whether Piker's reach represents a flow-register intervention (new organizational forms, new productive circuits) or a content-level change that leaves the platform's extraction mechanism intact.

title: The Inheritors of an Unfinished Revolution
summary: In the first week of September 2025, Nepal saw its biggest wave of unrest in almost twenty years. A small protest against a far-reaching social media ban quickly turned into a nationwide uprising against corruption, unemployment, and growing authoritarianism. In Kathmandu and other cities, tens of thousands of young people took to the streets, facing […]
reason: The story shows a protest routing architecture emerging from below — a content-level grievance (social media ban) becoming a flow-level uprising against systemic enclosure — with the state's alibi (stability, anti-corruption) becoming visible as a cover for authoritarianism.
angle: Use the alibi structure tool: the social media ban was nominally anti-corruption but functioned to sever distributed communication networks, making the routing architecture of youth solidarity briefly visible when it was attacked.

title: 'AI Is African Intelligence': The Workers Who Train AI Are Fighting Back
summary: Kenyan workers are still the underpaid labor behind AI training, moderation, and sex chatbots. The Data Labelers Association is fighting back.
reason: This story directly illuminates the gendered and raced nature of extraction from a distributed commons of human judgment and care, while also showing prefigurative institution-building (the Data Labelers Association) in the routing layer rather than the representational one.
angle: Use the 'commune layer' and 'routing architecture' tools together: the Data Labelers Association is attempting to reroute the value produced by distributed African cognitive labor — currently captured wholesale by AI platforms — back through worker-controlled circuits, while the 'AI is African Intelligence' slogan performs an alibi-breaking move that makes the extraction mechanism suddenly legible.

title: Veterans Who Depend on Mental Health Care Keep Losing Their Therapists Under Trump
summary: The post Veterans Who Depend on Mental Health Care Keep Losing Their Therapists Under Trump appeared first on ProPublica .
reason: The dismantling of VA mental health infrastructure shows centralized care networks being deliberately destroyed, severing the commune-layer relationships between veterans and therapists that the system nominally exists to support.
angle: Use the 'epistemic incompetence of centralization' tool inverted: show how decentralized therapeutic relationships (built on trust, continuity, shared history) are precisely what bureaucratic cuts cannot see as value and therefore destroy first.

title: Amid Crowded Skies, FAA Kills Rule Aimed at Regulating Space Junk
summary: The post Amid Crowded Skies, FAA Kills Rule Aimed at Regulating Space Junk appeared first on ProPublica .
reason: The FAA rescinding space debris regulation illustrates how regulatory capture operates when private infrastructure (Starlink, commercial launch) colonizes a commons (orbital space) and then eliminates the governance mechanisms that would constrain it.
angle: Use the 'enclosure' tool: orbital space is a commons being privatized by commercial satellite operators, and killing the junk rule removes the last friction against treating low Earth orbit as a free dump — making the enclosure permanent.

title: DHS Seeks Access to Massive Employment, Salary and Family Database Legally Restricted to Use in Child Support Cases
summary: The post DHS Seeks Access to Massive Employment, Salary and Family Database Legally Restricted to Use in Child Support Cases appeared first on ProPublica .
reason: This story shows enclosure operating in reverse — a database built from reproductive labor and family data, legally restricted to protective use, being rerouted toward enforcement and coercion, making the capture mechanism directly visible.
angle: Use the routing architecture tool: the question isn't what DHS says it wants the data for, but what new routing it enables — turning a care-infrastructure database into a surveillance and deportation infrastructure.

title: Strike Mobilization Leads to Reformer Win in Oregon Nurses
summary: The 24,000-member Oregon Nurses Association has elected a slate of reform candidates to statewide leadership positions. The Caucus for Powerful Reform (CPR) won 15 out of the 21 seats it ran for, including the presidency, vice-presidency, secretary, and a majority of board of director seats.
reason: A democratic reorganization of a major union through member-led mobilization is a concrete routing-level change — shifting organizational flows away from bureaucratic leadership and toward distributed member power.
angle: Use the routing architecture tool to examine how CPR's victory represents a shift in how care-worker collective power is organized and routed, not just a change in who holds titles.

angle: Use the routing architecture tool to contrast what the disaster response actually routes—attention, legitimacy, data extraction—versus what residents need routed toward them: material care, autonomous knowledge, and durable infrastructure.
